config.title: Tropical Islands from Berlin: FAQ
config.subtitle: Trains, tickets, prices and honest expectations for the tropical dome an hour south of Berlin.

items.q: How do I get to Tropical Islands from Berlin without a car?
items.a: Take the RE2 or RB24 regional train to Brand Tropical Islands station, about 45 minutes from Berlin Ostkreuz, then the free shuttle bus that waits for arriving trains. In 2026 the trains leave from Ostkreuz, Gesundbrunnen or Schöneweide rather than the Stadtbahn stations, because the central viaduct is closed to regional trains from 14 June to 12 December 2026. Check the VBB app on the day for your exact departure.

items.q: Is Tropical Islands covered by the Deutschlandticket?
items.a: Yes. RE2 and RB24 are regional trains inside the VBB network, so the €63/month Deutschlandticket covers the full ride to Brand Tropical Islands, including the S-Bahn to Ostkreuz. Without it, a group of up to five can share a €36.50 Brandenburg-Berlin-Ticket, which on weekdays is valid from 09:00.

items.q: How much does a Tropical Islands day cost?
items.a: Online day tickets start around €34.90 for adults, €29.90 for teens and children from four; children up to three are free. The same ticket at the door costs €55.90 or more, so booking online first is the single biggest saving. The textile-free Sauna & Spa area costs extra (from about €39.90 as a combined online ticket), and evening tickets from 18:00 start around €29.90.

items.q: Is Tropical Islands worth it as a day trip from Berlin?
items.a: For families, for a rainy or cold day, or for anyone curious about swimming under a hangar roof 107 m high, yes. It is a full-day commitment: with travel you should plan around 9 to 10 hours door to door. If your Berlin time is short and the weather is good, a city day plus a lake usually beats it; the dome is the plan for the day the weather breaks.

items.q: Do I need to book Tropical Islands tickets in advance?
items.a: You do not have to, but you should: online prices are date-based and roughly €20 per adult cheaper than the door price, and busy school-holiday and weekend dates can sell out. Book on the official site, then treat the 08:00 to 23:30 day as flexible; the ticket is for the date, not a time slot.
